questions.question: 9 (a) Which reagents are mixed with hydrochloric acid to produce the benzenediazonium ion? N + N benzenediazonium ion (1) A C 6 H 5 NH 2 and NaNO 2 B C 6 H 5 NO 2 and NaNO 2 C C 6 H 5 NO 2 and NaNO 3 D C 6 H 5 NH 2 and NaNO 3 (b) Which reagents and conditions are used to prepare the yellow dye 4‑phenyldiazenylphenol from the benzenediazoni
questions.text: 9 (a) Which reagents are mixed with hydrochloric acid to produce the benzenediazonium ion? N + N benzenediazonium ion (1) A C 6 H 5 NH 2 and NaNO 2 B C 6 H 5 NO 2 and NaNO 2 C C 6 H 5 NO 2 and NaNO 3 D C 6 H 5 NH 2 and NaNO 3 (b) Which reagents and conditions are used to prepare the yellow dye 4‑phenyldiazenylphenol from the benzenediazoni

parts.answer: The only correct answer is D (phenol dissolved in an alkaline solution at 5°C) A is incorrect because the reaction will not take place in acid and the temperature is too high B is incorrect because the temperature is too high C is incorrect because the reaction will not take place in acid (1) Number Answer Mark 10 The only correct answer is C (dissolve in the minimum volume of hot solvent, filter to remove the insoluble impurities, then cool and filter to remove the soluble impurities) A is incorrect because the hot solvent should be used first B incorrect because there is no cold filtering D is incorrect because the first filtration removes the insoluble impurities and the second filtration removes the soluble impurities (1) Number Answer Mark 11 The only correct answer is B (27.0 g) A is incorrect because the mass of phenol has been multiplied by 0.8 and 0.75 C is incorrect because only the yield in the second step has been used D is incorrect because only the yield in the first step has been used (1) Number Answer Mark 12 The only correct answer is D (C H ) A is incorrect because this is an empirical not molecular formula B is incorrect because the molar mass of H has been used instead of H C is incorrect because this is the closest formula using the ratio of mass of compound to mass of carbon dioxide (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A (15.6 and 9.6 ) B is incorrect because 0.5 moles of O has been used not 0.65 C is incorrect because 0.9 moles of O has been used not 0.65 and the moles of C H have not been multiplied by 4 D is incorrect because the volume of gaseous H O has been used not CO (1) Number Answer Mark The only correct answer is B A is incorrect because (poly)ethenol only has one OH in each repeat unit C is incorrect because (poly)ethenol only has one OH in each repeat unit D is incorrect because (poly)ethenol only has one OH in each repeat unit (1) Number Answer Mark 15 The only correct answer is A (H N(CH ) NH and HOOC(CH ) COOH) B is

The only correct answer is C ( compound Y) A is incorrect because it would give 3 proton NMR peaks and 3 C peaks B is incorrect because it would give 3 proton NMR peaks and 3 C peaks D is incorrect because it would give 3 proton NMR peaks and 3 C peaks (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because the amine group has not been protonated B is incorrect because this is the zwitterion and would not be present at pH 2 C is incorrect because the amide oxygen would not be protonated (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because on the adjacent C there is only one H so it would produce a doublet C is incorrect because on the adjacent C there is only one H so it would produce a doublet D is incorrect because on the adjacent C there is only one H so it would produce a doublet (1) Section B Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • A (saturated/concentrated solution of) potassium nitrate / KNO • B platinum /Pt • C (solution containing) iron(II) sulfate / FeSO and iron(III) sulfate / Fe (SO ) • concentration 1 mol dm –3 / 1M with respect to Fe 2+ and Fe 3+ or 1 mol dm –3 of FeSO and 0.5 mol dm –3 Fe (SO ) (1) (1) (1) (1) Allow sodium nitrate / NaNO / sodium chloride / NaCl/potassium chloride/KCl Allow black Pt Accept iron nitrates and chlorides Allow just Fe 2+ and Fe 3+ If name and formula are given both must be correct but only penalise once. One ion and its correct concentration this will score 1 mark. Ignore any reference to pressure Ignore any state symbols (4) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • ions can flow through a salt bridge (but not through a wire) Allow the ions can move/pass Allow ions cannot flow through the wire Ignore to balance the ions Ignore wire will interfere with the reaction/products/cell Do not award electrons can flow through the salt bridge ions can travel through the wire (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • correct species • correct direction and balancing (1) (1) Example of equation Zn + 2Fe 3+ Zn 2+ + 2Fe 2+ Ignore state symbols even
